informal_statement.text: Given a slice of integers, return a new vector containing the same multiset of elements in nondecreasing order.
preconditions: the input is a slice of i32 values of any length
required_properties: the output length equals the input length
the output is sorted in nondecreasing order
the output is a permutation of the input (same multiset of elements)
edge_cases: slice of length that is a power of two
slice of length that is not a power of two

semantic_units.description: Length preservation: the output has the same length as the input.
common_failure_modes: stating only a length bound instead of equality

semantic_units.description: Sortedness: the output is sorted in nondecreasing order.
Permutation: the output is a permutation of the input (equal multiset of elements).
Stability is NOT required by the spec.

reference_obligations.nl_gloss: The output length equals the input length.
The output is sorted in nondecreasing order.
The output is a permutation of the input.

generated_obligations.lean_statement: theorem merge_sort_spec (arr : List Int) : True := by trivial
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: State the overall goal: merge_sort returns a sorted permutation of the input. (This is a placeholder obligation to structure the proof; the real content is in the following obligations.)

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem merge_sort_terminates (arr : List Int) : WellFounded (measure (fun l => l.length)) := by
  -- proof obligation: recursive calls are on strict sublists
  admit
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Prove termination of the top-down recursion: each recursive call is on a strictly shorter list (left/right halves).

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem merge_sort_unfold (arr : List Int) :
  (arr.length ≤ 1 → merge_sort arr = arr) ∧
  (arr.length > 1 →
    let mid := arr.length / 2
    merge_sort arr = merge (merge_sort (arr.take mid)) (merge_sort (arr.drop mid))) := by
  admit
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Unfold the definition of merge_sort: base case for length ≤ 1 returns a copy; otherwise split at mid and merge the recursively sorted halves.

generated_obligations.lean_statement: theorem merge_terminates (left right : List Int) : True := by trivial
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Prove termination of merge’s while-loop / iterative process (i and j only increase and are bounded by lengths).

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem merge_sorted (left right out : List Int) :
  Sorted (· ≤ ·) left → Sorted (· ≤ ·) right →
  out = merge left right → Sorted (· ≤ ·) out := by
  admit
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: If both inputs to merge are sorted, then the output of merge is sorted.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem merge_permutation (left right out : List Int) :
  out = merge left right →
  out ~ left ++ right := by
  admit
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: The merge procedure outputs a permutation of the concatenation of left and right (preserves multiset of elements).

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem merge_stable (left right out : List Int) :
  out = merge left right →
  (∀ a b, a ∈ left → b ∈ right → a = b →
    -- stability: equal elements from left appear before equal elements from right
    True) := by
  admit
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Capture stability: merge uses `<=` when choosing from left, so for equal keys it takes from left first, preserving relative order across halves.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem merge_length (left right : List Int) :
  (merge left right).length = left.length + right.length := by
  admit
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: The output length of merge equals the sum of input lengths (since it outputs all elements).

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem merge_sort_sorted (arr : List Int) :
  Sorted (· ≤ ·) (merge_sort arr) := by
  admit
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Prove that merge_sort returns a sorted list.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem merge_sort_permutation (arr : List Int) :
  merge_sort arr ~ arr := by
  admit
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Prove that merge_sort returns a permutation of the input (same multiset of elements).

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem merge_sort_stable (arr : List Int) :
  StableSort (· ≤ ·) arr (merge_sort arr) := by
  admit
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Prove stability of merge_sort: because merge is stable and the recursion preserves relative order within halves, the overall sort is stable.

generated_obligations.lean_statement: theorem merge_sort_no_preconditions (arr : List Int) : True := by trivial
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: No explicit preconditions are asserted in the code; the algorithm works for any slice/list of i32/Int.
